practicals.title: Listening Skill Assessment: Audio Text Comprehension
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and answer comprehension questions, assessing decoding and inference skills.

practicals.title: Speaking Skill Assessment: Oral Interview Simulation
practicals.details: Students conduct simulated interviews based on the story, evaluated for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Reported Speech Transformation
practicals.details: Students transform direct speech into reported speech in context, focusing on accuracy and grammatical conventions.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Descriptive Paragraph from Visual Cue
practicals.details: Students write a descriptive paragraph based on a visual cue, assessing creative expression and appropriate style.

practicals.title: Reading Practical: Unseen Passage Comprehension
practicals.details: Students read an unseen passage and answer questions assessing comprehension, analysis, and vocabulary.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Practical: Role Play Assessment
practicals.details: Students perform a role play assessed on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and language accuracy.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Gap Filling and Editing
practicals.details: Students complete gap-filling and editing exercises to demonstrate high professional grasp of grammatical items.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Formal Letter Composition
practicals.details: Students write a formal letter based on a given situation, assessing appropriate format and fluency.

practicals.title: Listening Practical: Audio Text Retelling
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

practicals.title: Speaking Practical: Debate Participation
practicals.details: Students participate in a debate, assessed for interactive competence, fluency, and logical argumentation.

activities.title: Role Play: The Fun They Had
activities.details: Students enact a scene from the story to explore themes of education and technology, focusing on oral communication and character interpretation.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students recite the poem focusing on rhythm, intonation, and emotional expression to demonstrate literary appreciation.

activities.title: Dramatization: The Snake and the Mirror
activities.details: Students perform a skit based on the story to enhance understanding of plot, character dynamics, and creative expression.

activities.title: Group Discussion: Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students engage in a debate on the themes of loss and human nature, practicing logical argumentation and listening skills.

activities.title: Listening Activity: Audio Text Retelling
activities.details: Students listen to an audio recording of the story and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

activities.title: Visual Interpretation: Chart to Dialogue
activities.details: Students interpret a visual chart or sketch related to the story and create a dialogue based on the information provided.

activities.title: Interactive Speaking: Role Play in Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students simulate real-life scenarios involving the characters to practice communicative competence and social interaction.

activities.title: Debate: The Proposal (Play)
activities.details: Students participate in a structured debate on the themes of marriage and social pressure, enhancing critical thinking and speaking skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking: News Report Simulation
activities.details: Students create and present a news report based on the story, focusing on fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

activities.title: Poetry Appreciation: Imagery Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze the imagery and poetic devices in the poem through guided discussion and oral presentation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Skit on The Fun They Had
projects.details: Students create a skit incorporating art elements to dramatize the story, assessing creativity and collaborative learning.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Literary Appreciation Diary
projects.details: Students maintain a diary reflecting on themes, characters, and poetic devices across multiple poems, fostering independent reflection.

projects.title: Research Project: Contemporary Issues in Literature
projects.details: Students research contemporary issues related to the story and present findings through a written report and oral presentation.

projects.title: Creative Writing Project: Story Extension
projects.details: Students write an extended version of the story, focusing on creativity, imagination, and extrapolation beyond the text.

projects.title: Multilingualism Project: Indian Literature Appreciation
projects.details: Students explore Indian languages and literature, creating a comparative analysis project to develop sensitivity to diverse cultures.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Project: Oral Presentation Series
projects.details: Students deliver a series of oral presentations on topics related to the story, assessed for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

projects.title: Visual Interpretation Project: Graph to Report
projects.details: Students transcode information from a graph or chart into a descriptive report, integrating visual and written communication skills.

projects.title: Collaborative Project: Drama Production

practicals.title: Listening Skill Assessment: Audio Text Comprehension
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and answer comprehension questions, assessing decoding and inference skills.

practicals.title: Speaking Skill Assessment: Oral Interview Simulation
practicals.details: Students conduct simulated interviews based on the story, evaluated for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Reported Speech Transformation
practicals.details: Students transform direct speech into reported speech in context, focusing on accuracy and grammatical conventions.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Descriptive Paragraph from Visual Cue
practicals.details: Students write a descriptive paragraph based on a visual cue, assessing creative expression and appropriate style.

practicals.title: Reading Practical: Unseen Passage Comprehension
practicals.details: Students read an unseen passage and answer questions assessing comprehension, analysis, and vocabulary.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Practical: Role Play Assessment
practicals.details: Students perform a role play assessed on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and language accuracy.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Gap Filling and Editing
practicals.details: Students complete gap-filling and editing exercises to demonstrate high professional grasp of grammatical items.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Formal Letter Composition
practicals.details: Students write a formal letter based on a given situation, assessing appropriate format and fluency.

practicals.title: Listening Practical: Audio Text Retelling
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

practicals.title: Speaking Practical: Debate Participation
practicals.details: Students participate in a debate, assessed for interactive competence, fluency, and logical argumentation.

activities.title: Role Play: The Fun They Had
activities.details: Students enact a scene from the story to explore themes of education and technology, focusing on oral communication and character interpretation.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students recite the poem focusing on rhythm, intonation, and emotional expression to demonstrate literary appreciation.

activities.title: Dramatization: The Snake and the Mirror
activities.details: Students perform a skit based on the story to enhance understanding of plot, character dynamics, and creative expression.

activities.title: Group Discussion: Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students engage in a debate on the themes of loss and human nature, practicing logical argumentation and listening skills.

activities.title: Listening Activity: Audio Text Retelling
activities.details: Students listen to an audio recording of the story and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

activities.title: Visual Interpretation: Chart to Dialogue
activities.details: Students interpret a visual chart or sketch related to the story and create a dialogue based on the information provided.

activities.title: Interactive Speaking: Role Play in Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students simulate real-life scenarios involving the characters to practice communicative competence and social interaction.

activities.title: Debate: The Proposal (Play)
activities.details: Students participate in a structured debate on the themes of marriage and social pressure, enhancing critical thinking and speaking skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking: News Report Simulation
activities.details: Students create and present a news report based on the story, focusing on fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

activities.title: Poetry Appreciation: Imagery Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze the imagery and poetic devices in the poem through guided discussion and oral presentation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Skit on The Fun They Had
projects.details: Students create a skit incorporating art elements to dramatize the story, assessing creativity and collaborative learning.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Literary Appreciation Diary
projects.details: Students maintain a diary reflecting on themes, characters, and poetic devices across multiple poems, fostering independent reflection.

projects.title: Research Project: Contemporary Issues in Literature
projects.details: Students research contemporary issues related to the story and present findings through a written report and oral presentation.

projects.title: Creative Writing Project: Story Extension
projects.details: Students write an extended version of the story, focusing on creativity, imagination, and extrapolation beyond the text.

projects.title: Multilingualism Project: Indian Literature Appreciation
projects.details: Students explore Indian languages and literature, creating a comparative analysis project to develop sensitivity to diverse cultures.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Project: Oral Presentation Series
projects.details: Students deliver a series of oral presentations on topics related to the story, assessed for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

projects.title: Visual Interpretation Project: Graph to Report
projects.details: Students transcode information from a graph or chart into a descriptive report, integrating visual and written communication skills.

projects.title: Collaborative Project: Drama Production
projects.details: Students collaborate to produce a full drama performance, assessing teamwork, creative expression, and oral communication.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Grammar in Context
projects.details: Students compile a portfolio of grammar exercises applied to literary texts, focusing on accuracy and contextual usage.

projects.title: Interdisciplinary Project: Literature and Social Issues
projects.details: Students connect literary themes to social issues, creating a project that combines reading, writing, and critical thinking.

practicals.title: Listening Skill Assessment: Audio Text Comprehension
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and answer comprehension questions, assessing decoding and inference skills.

practicals.title: Speaking Skill Assessment: Oral Interview Simulation
practicals.details: Students conduct simulated interviews based on the story, evaluated for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Reported Speech Transformation
practicals.details: Students transform direct speech into reported speech in context, focusing on accuracy and grammatical conventions.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Descriptive Paragraph from Visual Cue
practicals.details: Students write a descriptive paragraph based on a visual cue, assessing creative expression and appropriate style.

practicals.title: Reading Practical: Unseen Passage Comprehension
practicals.details: Students read an unseen passage and answer questions assessing comprehension, analysis, and vocabulary.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Practical: Role Play Assessment
practicals.details: Students perform a role play assessed on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and language accuracy.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Gap Filling and Editing
practicals.details: Students complete gap-filling and editing exercises to demonstrate high professional grasp of grammatical items.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Formal Letter Composition
practicals.details: Students write a formal letter based on a given situation, assessing appropriate format and fluency.

practicals.title: Listening Practical: Audio Text Retelling
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

practicals.title: Speaking Practical: Debate Participation
practicals.details: Students participate in a debate, assessed for interactive competence, fluency, and logical argumentation.

activities.title: Role Play: The Fun They Had
activities.details: Students enact a scene from the story to explore themes of education and technology, focusing on oral communication and character interpretation.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students recite the poem focusing on rhythm, intonation, and emotional expression to demonstrate literary appreciation.

activities.title: Dramatization: The Snake and the Mirror
activities.details: Students perform a skit based on the story to enhance understanding of plot, character dynamics, and creative expression.

activities.title: Group Discussion: Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students engage in a debate on the themes of loss and human nature, practicing logical argumentation and listening skills.

activities.title: Listening Activity: Audio Text Retelling
activities.details: Students listen to an audio recording of the story and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

activities.title: Visual Interpretation: Chart to Dialogue
activities.details: Students interpret a visual chart or sketch related to the story and create a dialogue based on the information provided.

activities.title: Interactive Speaking: Role Play in Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students simulate real-life scenarios involving the characters to practice communicative competence and social interaction.

activities.title: Debate: The Proposal (Play)
activities.details: Students participate in a structured debate on the themes of marriage and social pressure, enhancing critical thinking and speaking skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking: News Report Simulation
activities.details: Students create and present a news report based on the story, focusing on fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

activities.title: Poetry Appreciation: Imagery Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze the imagery and poetic devices in the poem through guided discussion and oral presentation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Skit on The Fun They Had
projects.details: Students create a skit incorporating art elements to dramatize the story, assessing creativity and collaborative learning.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Literary Appreciation Diary
projects.details: Students maintain a diary reflecting on themes, characters, and poetic devices across multiple poems, fostering independent reflection.

projects.title: Research Project: Contemporary Issues in Literature
projects.details: Students research contemporary issues related to the story and present findings through a written report and oral presentation.

projects.title: Creative Writing Project: Story Extension
projects.details: Students write an extended version of the story, focusing on creativity, imagination, and extrapolation beyond the text.

projects.title: Multilingualism Project: Indian Literature Appreciation
projects.details: Students explore Indian languages and literature, creating a comparative analysis project to develop sensitivity to diverse cultures.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Project: Oral Presentation Series
projects.details: Students deliver a series of oral presentations on topics related to the story, assessed for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

projects.title: Visual Interpretation Project: Graph to Report
projects.details: Students transcode information from a graph or chart into a descriptive report, integrating visual and written communication skills.

projects.title: Collaborative Project: Drama Production
projects.details: Students collaborate to produce a full drama performance, assessing teamwork, creative expression, and oral communication.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Grammar in Context
projects.details: Students compile a portfolio of grammar exercises applied to literary texts, focusing on accuracy and contextual usage.

projects.title: Interdisciplinary Project: Literature and Social Issues
projects.details: Students connect literary themes to social issues, creating a project that combines reading, writing, and critical thinking.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Two unseen passages (Discursive 400-450 words, Case-based factual 200-250 words with visual input). Total 600-700 words. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-Verb Concord, Reported Speech. Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (10 out of 12 questions).
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) OR Formal Letter (Class X); Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words) OR Analytical Paragraph (Class X). One out of two.
Reference to Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Short & Long Answer Questions: From Beehive/First Flight and Moments/Footprints Without Feet. Short answers (40-50 words), Long answers (100-120 words).
Internal Assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills (5 Marks).
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary.
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy.
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying themes/messages.
internal_assessment: Integrated component of all four language skills.
Activities include Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
practical_assessment: Oral Assessment: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar/vocabulary).
Rubric parameters: Interaction, Fluency & Coherence, Pronunciation, Vocabulary & Grammar.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Assessment of Listening and Speaking Skills (ALS) under Subject Enrichment Activity.

competency_groups.title: Reading Comprehension & Analysis
competency_groups.description: Competencies related to decoding, interpreting, analyzing, inferring, and evaluating unseen passages including discursive and case-based factual texts with visual inputs.

competency_groups.title: Writing Skills & Grammar Application
competency_groups.description: Competencies involving creative expression, reasoning, justification, and the accurate application of grammatical conventions including tenses, modals, reported speech, and punctuation.
competencies: Illustrating ideas with appropriate style and tone
Applying correct format for letters and paragraphs
Applying grammatical conventions accurately (Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-verb concord, Reported speech)

competency_groups.title: Language Through Literature
competency_groups.description: Competencies focused on recalling, reasoning, appreciating literary conventions, identifying themes and sub-themes, understanding the writer's message, and extrapolating beyond the text.
competencies: Interpreting extracts from prose, poetry, and drama

competency_groups.title: Listening and Speaking Competencies
competency_groups.description: Competencies for interactive communication, fluency, pronunciation, and grammatical accuracy in oral settings including role plays, discussions, and presentations.

practicals.title: Listening Skill Assessment: Audio Text Comprehension
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and answer comprehension questions, assessing decoding and inference skills.

practicals.title: Speaking Skill Assessment: Oral Interview Simulation
practicals.details: Students conduct simulated interviews based on the story, evaluated for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Reported Speech Transformation
practicals.details: Students transform direct speech into reported speech in context, focusing on accuracy and grammatical conventions.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Descriptive Paragraph from Visual Cue
practicals.details: Students write a descriptive paragraph based on a visual cue, assessing creative expression and appropriate style.

practicals.title: Reading Practical: Unseen Passage Comprehension
practicals.details: Students read an unseen passage and answer questions assessing comprehension, analysis, and vocabulary.

practicals.title: Listening and Speaking Practical: Role Play Assessment
practicals.details: Students perform a role play assessed on interactive competence, fluency, pronunciation, and language accuracy.

practicals.title: Grammar Practical: Gap Filling and Editing
practicals.details: Students complete gap-filling and editing exercises to demonstrate high professional grasp of grammatical items.

practicals.title: Writing Practical: Formal Letter Composition
practicals.details: Students write a formal letter based on a given situation, assessing appropriate format and fluency.

practicals.title: Listening Practical: Audio Text Retelling
practicals.details: Students listen to an audio text and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

practicals.title: Speaking Practical: Debate Participation
practicals.details: Students participate in a debate, assessed for interactive competence, fluency, and logical argumentation.

activities.title: Role Play: The Fun They Had
activities.details: Students enact a scene from the story to explore themes of education and technology, focusing on oral communication and character interpretation.

activities.title: Poetry Recitation with Intonation
activities.details: Students recite the poem focusing on rhythm, intonation, and emotional expression to demonstrate literary appreciation.

activities.title: Dramatization: The Snake and the Mirror
activities.details: Students perform a skit based on the story to enhance understanding of plot, character dynamics, and creative expression.

activities.title: Group Discussion: Contemporary Issues
activities.details: Students engage in a debate on the themes of loss and human nature, practicing logical argumentation and listening skills.

activities.title: Listening Activity: Audio Text Retelling
activities.details: Students listen to an audio recording of the story and retell the contents, focusing on comprehension and oral narration.

activities.title: Visual Interpretation: Chart to Dialogue
activities.details: Students interpret a visual chart or sketch related to the story and create a dialogue based on the information provided.

activities.title: Interactive Speaking: Role Play in Real Life Situations
activities.details: Students simulate real-life scenarios involving the characters to practice communicative competence and social interaction.

activities.title: Debate: The Proposal (Play)
activities.details: Students participate in a structured debate on the themes of marriage and social pressure, enhancing critical thinking and speaking skills.

activities.title: Listening and Speaking: News Report Simulation
activities.details: Students create and present a news report based on the story, focusing on fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

activities.title: Poetry Appreciation: Imagery Analysis
activities.details: Students analyze the imagery and poetic devices in the poem through guided discussion and oral presentation.

projects.title: Art-Integrated Project: Skit on The Fun They Had
projects.details: Students create a skit incorporating art elements to dramatize the story, assessing creativity and collaborative learning.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Literary Appreciation Diary
projects.details: Students maintain a diary reflecting on themes, characters, and poetic devices across multiple poems, fostering independent reflection.

projects.title: Research Project: Contemporary Issues in Literature
projects.details: Students research contemporary issues related to the story and present findings through a written report and oral presentation.

projects.title: Creative Writing Project: Story Extension
projects.details: Students write an extended version of the story, focusing on creativity, imagination, and extrapolation beyond the text.

projects.title: Multilingualism Project: Indian Literature Appreciation
projects.details: Students explore Indian languages and literature, creating a comparative analysis project to develop sensitivity to diverse cultures.

projects.title: Listening and Speaking Project: Oral Presentation Series
projects.details: Students deliver a series of oral presentations on topics related to the story, assessed for fluency, pronunciation, and interactive competence.

projects.title: Visual Interpretation Project: Graph to Report
projects.details: Students transcode information from a graph or chart into a descriptive report, integrating visual and written communication skills.

projects.title: Collaborative Project: Drama Production
projects.details: Students collaborate to produce a full drama performance, assessing teamwork, creative expression, and oral communication.

projects.title: Portfolio Project: Grammar in Context
projects.details: Students compile a portfolio of grammar exercises applied to literary texts, focusing on accuracy and contextual usage.

projects.title: Interdisciplinary Project: Literature and Social Issues
projects.details: Students connect literary themes to social issues, creating a project that combines reading, writing, and critical thinking.

marks_distribution: Section A: Reading Skills - 20 Marks
Section B: Writing Skills and Grammar - 20 Marks
Section C: Language through Literature - 40 Marks
Internal Assessment (Listening & Speaking) - 5 Marks
question_paper_design: Reading Comprehension: Two unseen passages (Discursive 400-450 words, Case-based factual 200-250 words with visual input). Total 600-700 words. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Grammar: Determiners, Tenses, Modals, Subject-Verb Concord, Reported Speech. Gap Filling/Editing/Transformation (10 out of 12 questions).
Writing Skills: Descriptive Paragraph (100-120 words) OR Formal Letter (Class X); Story/Diary Entry (100-120 words) OR Analytical Paragraph (Class X). One out of two.
Reference to Context: Extracts from Drama/Prose and Poetry. MCQs/Objective/Very Short Answer.
Short & Long Answer Questions: From Beehive/First Flight and Moments/Footprints Without Feet. Short answers (40-50 words), Long answers (100-120 words).
Internal Assessment: Listening and Speaking Skills (5 Marks).
competency_weightage: Reading Comprehension: Conceptual understanding, decoding, analyzing, inferring, interpreting, vocabulary.
Writing Skills & Grammar: Creative expression, reasoning, justifying, appropriate style/tone/format, fluency, accuracy.
Language through Literature: Recalling, reasoning, appreciating, applying literary conventions, identifying themes/messages.
internal_assessment: Integrated component of all four language skills.
Activities include Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
practical_assessment: Oral Assessment: Interactive competence (Initiation & turn taking), Fluency (cohesion, coherence, speed), Pronunciation, Language (grammar/vocabulary).
Rubric parameters: Interaction, Fluency & Coherence, Pronunciation, Vocabulary & Grammar.
project_assessment: Art-integrated projects based on activities like Role Play, Skit, Dramatization.
Assessment of Listening and Speaking Skills (ALS) under Subject Enrichment Activity.
